## ThetaDataDx 13.0.0-rc.5

This release candidate renames the server-channel configuration around the data each channel serves (a breaking config change), adds a columnar Arrow streaming reader alongside the per-event callback, and lands a round of correctness and robustness hardening across the bindings. See the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/userFRM/ThetaDataDx/blob/v13.0.0-rc.5/CHANGELOG.md) for the full, itemized list.

### Highlights

- Channels are configured by what they serve. The historical channel and the streaming channel are now selected independently, each with its own production/staging (historical) or production/dev (streaming) selector, and the matching environment variables, server flags, and binding parameters are renamed to the historical/streaming names. This is a breaking change to configuration; the migration is a rename, and an unrecognized or cross-channel selector now fails with a clear error instead of falling back silently. See the Breaking changes in the CHANGELOG for the full mapping.
- A pull-based Arrow streaming reader. Alongside the existing per-event callback, you can now pull decoded streaming events as Arrow record batches with `batches()`: a `Stream` and a blocking iterator in Rust, a sync and async iterable and context manager in Python (handed to pyarrow zero-copy), an `AsyncIterable<RecordBatch>` in TypeScript, and a native `arrow::RecordBatchReader` in C++. Batch size, a flush interval, and a block-or-drop backpressure choice are all tunable, and every batch shares one fixed schema across the bindings. The callback delivery mode is unchanged.
- Robustness hardening. Two streaming-teardown hangs are fixed (a standalone FFI client whose callback re-entered a status read, and a TypeScript stop blocked behind a saturated callback queue), a channel-environment setter no longer wipes a custom configuration when a later check fails, config-file loading no longer panics or absorbs ambient environment overrides on a bad selector, and the CLI emits `null` for non-finite numbers to match the other frontends.
- Dependency and toolchain security updates. esbuild on the docs-site toolchain and memmap2, h2, webpki-roots, and rustls-platform-verifier in the Rust tree are bumped to clear advisories. None of these change a shipped SDK API.

### Installing

This is a pre-release, so the package managers will not pick it up unless you ask for it explicitly.

- npm: `npm install thetadatadx@next`
- PyPI: `pip install --pre thetadatadx`
- crates.io: pin `thetadatadx = "=13.0.0-rc.5"`

### Thanks

Thanks to everyone running the release candidates and sending feedback and patches. The config rename is a real change to muscle memory, so if anything in the migration trips you up, open an issue and we will sort it out. If you sent something in and you do not see your name, that is on us, not you, and we will fix the credit.
